name: content-fingerprint-v1-happy-path
description: Per-vault keyed file fingerprint (formats §10.2). HKDF info `dc-vault-v1/content-fingerprint`; fingerprint is base64 of HMAC-SHA256(subkey, plaintext_sha256).

name: content-fingerprint-v1-empty-plaintext
description: Empty-file plaintext_sha256 (e3b0c4...) still produces a stable fingerprint that's recognisable across implementations.

name: content-fingerprint-v1-different-master-key-diverges
description: Review §7.H2 — same plaintext_sha256 as the happy-path case but a different vault_master_key. The HMAC keying is the entire point: a different master_key MUST produce a different fingerprint (and a different subkey). diverges_from_b64 pins the inequality against the happy-path fingerprint so a future regression that ignored the master_key gets caught here in the cross-runtime parity gate.

name: content-fingerprint-v1-different-plaintext-diverges
description: Review §7.H2 — same vault_master_key as the happy-path case but a different plaintext_sha256. The MAC tag MUST mix the plaintext_sha256; without that, two distinct files would collide and §8.4 dedup decisions would be silently wrong.
